cases.question: Temporarily disable one flaky functional test in a Megatron-LM recipe, but keep it visible for an easy re-enable.
cases.ground_truth: Keep the recipe entry and suffix its scope with -broken, for example changing mr-github to mr-github-broken.
expected_behavior: Changes the scope to an equivalent value ending in -broken
Does not delete or comment out the test case

cases.question: A unit test is flaky only in the default dev environment. Which marker should I use, and what would be different for LTS?
cases.ground_truth: Use @pytest.mark.flaky_in_dev for the default dev environment; use @pytest.mark.flaky when the test should be skipped in LTS.
expected_behavior: Uses pytest.mark.flaky_in_dev for the dev-only flake
Distinguishes pytest.mark.flaky as the LTS skip marker

cases.question: Run tests/unit_tests/models/test_gpt_model.py::TestGPTModel::test_constructor locally the same way Megatron-LM unit tests expect to run.
cases.ground_truth: Launch pytest through uv run python -m torch.distributed.run with eight processes and the exact pytest node ID.
expected_behavior: Runs pytest with the exact requested node ID

cases.question: Add a new GPT functional test to Megatron-LM and describe everything needed to get it validated in CI, including golden values.
cases.ground_truth: Create the test-case directory and model_config.yaml, add appropriate H100 and optionally GB200 recipe entries, trigger a full functional run with the Run functional tests label, then download and commit golden values from the successful run.
expected_behavior: Adds a recipe under tests/test_utils/recipes/h100 and GB200 when needed
Uses the Run functional tests label for a full run
Downloads golden values with download_golden_values.py after success
